architecture.summary: This is a mechanistic interpretability study of transformer-based LLMs answering multi-hop queries. The system analyzes internal computation without modifying the model: it traces where bridge entities are resolved (early layers at intermediate token positions) and where final answers are resolved (later layers at final token position), verifies information propagation between these positions via attention, and introduces back-patching—a method that extracts hidden representations from later layers and reinjects them at earlier positions to test whether later layers lack necessary knowledge for second-hop resolution.

biblio.title: Hopping Too Late: Exploring the Limitations of Large Language Models on Multi-Hop Queries

claims.dataset: custom two-hop queries (82,020 total, derived from Wikidata)
claims.statement: The bridge entity is resolved in the early layers of the model at the position of the last token of the first hop.

claims.dataset: custom two-hop queries (82,020 total, derived from Wikidata)
claims.statement: The second hop is typically resolved in the last token of the prompt only in layers after the resolution of the first hop.
The bridge entity is successfully decoded from the first-hop position across models in 41%-78% of correctly answered cases using Patchscopes.
The target entity is decoded from the final token position in 71%-86% of cases where the model predicted correctly.
Critical information propagates from the first-hop position to the final token position in the majority of cases (detected via attention knockout, projection, or Patchscopes).

claims.dataset: custom two-hop queries (82,020 total, derived from Wikidata); cases where model correctly answers both hops in isolation but fails on full two-hop query
claims.statement: Back-patching correctly generates the answer for up to 66% of previously incorrect cases when an optimal source-target layer pair is chosen.

claims.dataset: custom two-hop queries (82,020 total, derived from Wikidata); cases where model correctly answers the two-hop query
claims.metric: back-patching success rate on correct cases
claims.statement: Back-patching maintains 100% correctness on cases the model already answered correctly across all models and token positions.

claims.dataset: custom two-hop queries (82,020 total, derived from Wikidata)
claims.statement: The bridge entity resolves later in incorrect cases than in correct cases, suggesting delayed first-hop resolution contributes to multi-hop query failure.
